goals.title: Project scaffold
goals.summary: Defines the initial package scaffold, typed schemas, JSONL archives, safety primitives, evaluator, and CLI surface.

goals.title: Code-improver loop
goals.summary: Runs the bounded propose, sandbox, evaluate, archive, and select loop for code-improver tasks.

goals.title: Research memory
goals.summary: Records structured research memory, including negative results, and retrieves it for later proposals.

goals.title: Promotion gates
goals.summary: Adds code-integrity, safety, reproducibility, and hidden-test gates before promotion.

goals.title: Meta-research loop
goals.summary: Adds the bounded outer loop that proposes reversible process improvements and validates them by fixed A/B benchmarks.

goals.title: Tiny-training autoresearch
goals.summary: Applies the same improvement loop to bounded pure-Python training configurations and validation loss.

goals.title: Provider abstraction
goals.summary: Introduces provider-agnostic model clients, tier config, and token/USD budget enforcement.

goals.title: Frontier research org
goals.summary: Runs the full model-backed research organization with typed roles, control-plane tools, and cross-model safety review.

goals.title: Research-shaped task suite
goals.summary: Adds research tasks, objective evaluation harnesses, reproducibility checks, and per-family summaries.

goals.title: Governance gate
goals.summary: Adds a default-deny human approval ledger for governed Tier 2 actions.

goals.title: Governed compute scale-up
goals.summary: Adds governed compute tiers, checkpointing, and guarded research evaluation with hard wall-clock and memory ceilings.

goals.title: Governed model-training
goals.summary: Adds governed offline weight-update experiments, artifact lineage, and separately approved deployment.

goals.title: Documentation consistency contract
goals.summary: Makes README, goal prompts, manifest entries, Self-improvement sections, and docs path privacy mechanically checked.

goals.title: Pricing audit and budget calibration
goals.summary: Adds config-level price override metadata, pricing-audit reporting, and ledger metadata for cost estimates.

goals.title: Hard resource isolation backend
goals.summary: Adds a sandbox backend abstraction: a portable local monitor with process-tree accounting and a Linux cgroup v2 hard-isolation backend with kernel-enforced memory/process ceilings, plus a compute-tier backend policy.

goals.title: Durable research store and query layer
goals.summary: Adds a storage interface over every append-only stream with a SQLite backend (schema migrations, idempotency keys, hash-chained tamper-evidence, JSONL export/import) alongside the default JSONL backend, plus storage CLI verbs and store-backed summaries.

goals.title: Research benchmark suite expansion
goals.summary: Expands the fixed benchmark suite across existing and new families with adversarial variants and richer per-family summaries.

goals.title: Provider operations and observability
goals.summary: Adds provider error taxonomy, bounded retries, request metadata, provider ops config, and ledger-backed observability reports.

goals.title: Governance identity and policy hardening
goals.summary: Implements typed operator identities, signed approval proofs, policy templates, two-person approval, governance packet export, and identity/policy ledger verification.

goals.title: Bounded operational pilot and cost-per-promotion report
goals.summary: Implements a fixed budget-capped operational pilot plan, command transcript generation, and a cost-per-promotion report over research/model-call ledgers.

goals.title: Conversational operating interface in Claude Code and Codex
goals.summary: Operates siro as a dialogue hosted in Claude Code or Codex through host-specific repo-local skills (no REPL); adds thin CLI affordances — a global --json structured output on the read-only summaries and a side-effect-free --dry-run plan path that prints command/tier/governance implications — so the skills read state and propose-before-acting within the existing governance bounds.

goals.title: Domain-pack interface and evaluator adapter
goals.summary: Formalizes the implicit task/evaluator convention into a typed EvaluatorAdapter plus a registrable domain-pack layout (tasks, prompts, references, tool whitelist), selected by config; reseats the existing ML families as packs/ml/ to prove the interface. Refactor only — no new domain.

goals.title: Mathematics proof-search pack (Lean)
goals.summary: First non-ML pack: a Regime-A (formal/exact) mathematics domain whose evaluator is a Lean proof checker run offline; candidates are proofs scored by verification plus proof length/dependency count, with the theorem statement read-only to agents.

goals.title: Statistical reproducibility gate
goals.summary: Generalizes the reproducibility/promotion gate from exact rerun agreement to a regime spectrum (exact / seeded-deterministic / statistical), adding a noise-aware policy that promotes only when the oriented gain clears a confidence bound across fixed seeded reruns — unlocking Regime B without relaxing 'no promotion on noise'.

goals.title: Chip-design pack
goals.summary: Chip-design domain whose candidates are RTL/synthesis recipes: correctness via offline formal equivalence against a controller-owned reference (Regime A) gates a power/performance/area objective measured by synthesis (Regime B, promoted under the Goal 24 statistical gate).

goals.title: Governed external-experiment boundary
goals.summary: Adds an EXTERNAL_EXPERIMENT GovernedAction and a propose → approve → execute → ingest lifecycle so Regime-C sciences (wet-lab, fabrication, instruments, paid compute) feed signed, human-approved results back into the loop while the execution plane never reaches the outside world.

goals.title: Drug and life-science pack
goals.summary: Capstone two-stage pack: cheap offline in-silico screening (Regime B, pinned surrogate fixtures) ranks candidates and gates a small number of human-approved wet-lab confirmations (Regime C via Goal 26); screening-before-confirmation keeps costly, irreversible assays few and high-value.
